title: Responsible agriculture must adapt to the wetland character of mid-latitude peatlands
geometry_note: Conceptual/review paper; no single study region. Use as global/evidence node.
study_design: Review / conceptual synthesis
land_use_system: responsible agriculture; paludiculture; transition systems
key_finding_short: Agricultural peatlands should be managed according to their wetland character; water table is central and transition systems may lower adoption barriers.
south_germany_relevance: Strong framing source for productive dairy/forage peatland regions where abrupt full restoration may be difficult.
caveat: Review/conceptual synthesis; not a regional farm-level or hydrological model.

title: Agriculture on wet peatlands: The sustainability potential of paludiculture
geometry_note: Review paper; no single point geometry.
study_design: Review
land_use_system: low-intensity paludiculture; high-intensity paludiculture; grazing; harvesting
key_finding_short: Differentiates low- and high-intensity paludiculture; low-intensity use includes grazing and harvesting of grasses, sedges and reed.
south_germany_relevance: Useful for broadening the framing beyond specialised paludiculture crops toward wet grassland use.
caveat: Bibliographic metadata/DOI still requires final validation.

title: Wet peatland utilisation for climate protection – An international survey of paludiculture innovation
geometry_note: International survey; use as network/value-chain node rather than one location.
study_design: Exploratory international survey / innovation analysis
land_use_system: paludiculture initiatives; biomass; construction; energy; substrate; fodder; food
key_finding_short: Paludiculture initiatives are diverse internationally but often small, science-driven and not yet economically self-supporting.
south_germany_relevance: Supports the argument that product pathways need market and value-chain development, not only agronomic feasibility.
caveat: Exploratory survey; not strong evidence for profitability or GHG effects.

title: Production in peatlands: Comparing ecosystem services of different land use options following conventional farming
geometry_note: Country-level point for Dutch peatland system comparison.
study_design: System/model comparison of land-use alternatives along a water-table gradient
land_use_system: conventional dairy; organic dairy; low-input grazing/mowing; paludiculture
key_finding_short: Compares conventional/organic dairy, low-input grazing/mowing and paludiculture along a water-table gradient.
south_germany_relevance: Strong for visualising transition options between intensive grassland systems and full wetland restoration.
caveat: Model/system comparison; not a real farm-level dataset.

title: State of Knowledge on UK Agricultural Peatlands for Food Production and the Net Zero Transition
region: UK lowland agricultural peatlands, especially the Fens
geometry_note: Approximate point for the English Fens / UK lowland agricultural peat context.
study_design: State-of-knowledge / workshop synthesis
land_use_system: food production; lowland agricultural peat; net zero transition
key_finding_short: Productive agricultural peatlands are important for food, income and water infrastructure; full rewetting can be socially and practically difficult.
south_germany_relevance: Useful analogue for productive agricultural peat regions where mitigation must be balanced with existing farming systems.
caveat: UK/Fens-specific and more synthesis/positioning than primary empirical evidence.

title: Measures to reduce land subsidence and greenhouse gas emissions in peatlands: A Dutch case study
geometry_note: Country-level point for Dutch case study.
study_design: Measures comparison / synthesis of technical and alternative land-use options
land_use_system: technical water management; paludiculture; alternative land uses
key_finding_short: Compares 27 technical measures and alternative land uses across 15 effect dimensions; technical measures are lower-risk but may be less transformative.
south_germany_relevance: Excellent source for visualising trade-offs between farmer risk, GHG mitigation, subsidence and land-use transformation.
caveat: Dutch case; transfer depends on peatland type, water management infrastructure and farm systems.

title: Shallow drainage of agricultural peatlands without land-use change: Have your peat and eat it too
region: Dutch agricultural peatlands / dairy grassland context
geometry_note: Country-level point; exact study region to validate from full text if needed.
study_design: Study of shallow drainage without land-use change
land_use_system: agricultural peatland; shallow drainage; grassland
key_finding_short: Water-table management without reduced land-use intensity may provide limited biodiversity, biogeochemical or CO2 mitigation benefits.
south_germany_relevance: Important warning against overselling partial hydrological measures without changes in management intensity.
caveat: Context-specific; should be interpreted with Dutch grassland and water-management conditions in mind.

title: The effect of raised groundwater tables on load-bearing capacity of peat grassland on a dairy farm
geometry_note: Country-level point; exact farm coordinates not used for privacy/precision.
study_design: Empirical field study on load-bearing capacity under raised groundwater
land_use_system: peat grassland; dairy farm; raised groundwater
key_finding_short: Raised groundwater tables can reduce load-bearing capacity; effects depend on soil moisture, season and management.
south_germany_relevance: Directly relevant to trafficability concerns in dairy/forage peat grassland systems.
caveat: Dutch dairy farm context; machinery systems and soils may differ in South Germany.

title: Evaluating peat pasture performance under elevated groundwater conditions
geometry_note: Country-level point; exact region to validate from full metadata.
study_design: Empirical or model-supported evaluation of peat pasture under elevated groundwater
land_use_system: peat pasture; raised groundwater; dairy grassland
key_finding_short: Raising groundwater from roughly 50 to 20 cm below surface reduced dry matter yield by about 9% while maintaining relatively high grass production and similar quality.
south_germany_relevance: Very strong transfer source for dairy/grassland-oriented peat regions.
caveat: DOI and final bibliographic metadata require validation; Dutch peat pasture systems are not identical to Upper Swabia.

title: Farm-Level Effects of Emissions Tax and Adjustable Drainage on Peatlands
region: Finnish cereal and dairy farms on peat soils
geometry_note: Country-level point for Finnish farm-level modelling.
study_design: Farm-level modelling of emissions tax and adjustable drainage
land_use_system: cereal farms; dairy farms; adjustable drainage
key_finding_short: Models incentives that may move farms toward water-table management; income losses may need compensation beyond production decisions.
south_germany_relevance: Useful for comparing CO2 premium / compensation approaches and farm-level economic exposure.
caveat: Finnish farm structures and policy context differ from South Germany; DOI to validate.

title: Rewetting on agricultural peatlands can offer cost effective greenhouse gas reduction at the national level
geometry_note: Country-level point for national Finnish scenario study.
study_design: National scenario / cost-effectiveness analysis
land_use_system: controlled drainage; paludiculture; restoration; afforestation
key_finding_short: Large-scale mitigation on agricultural peatlands can be cost-effective nationally but causes farm income losses and requires compensation.
south_germany_relevance: Supports distinction between societal abatement efficiency and uneven farm-level income effects.
caveat: National Finnish setting; DOI/final metadata to validate.

title: Regulation of reed (Phragmites australis) by water buffalo grazing: Use in coastal conservation
geometry_note: Approximate northern/coastal Germany point; exact site to validate if needed.
study_design: Case / management study on water buffalo grazing and reed regulation
land_use_system: water buffalo grazing; reed regulation; wet grassland
key_finding_short: Moderate water buffalo grazing can reduce reed dominance and support more open vegetation structures.
south_germany_relevance: Conceptually relevant for wet, reed-dominated or difficult-to-machine sites, but not a universal production model.
caveat: Coastal conservation context; not directly transferable to dairy/biogas production regions.

title: Examination of the Effects of Domestic Water Buffalo (Bubalus bubalis) Grazing on Wetland and Dry Grassland Habitats
geometry_note: Country-level point for Hungarian habitat management study.
study_design: Management study on water buffalo grazing in wetland and dry grassland habitats
land_use_system: domestic water buffalo grazing; wetland; dry grassland
key_finding_short: Water buffalo grazing can suppress undesirable/invasive species such as Solidago gigantea and alter grassland structure.
south_germany_relevance: Useful for robust grazing and habitat-management scenarios on wet mosaic landscapes.
caveat: Habitat-management focus, not a farm-economic study.

title: Comparison of GHG emissions from annual crops in rotation on drained temperate agricultural peatland with production of reed canary grass in paludiculture using an LCA approach
geometry_note: Approximate point for northern/central Denmark study sites.
study_design: Life Cycle Assessment comparing drained annual crops with reed canary grass paludiculture
land_use_system: annual crops; reed canary grass paludiculture
key_finding_short: Reed canary grass on wet/shallow-drained peat reduced GHG emissions compared with deeply drained annual cropping, depending strongly on achieved water table.
south_germany_relevance: Strong biomass-pathway evidence for agricultural peatland transition scenarios.
caveat: LCA and Danish sites; product-use assumptions and water-table performance must be checked for South Germany.

title: Common Reed for Thatching in Northern Germany: Estimating the Market Potential of Reed of Regional Origin
geometry_note: Approximate point for northern Germany reed value-chain context.
study_design: Market/value-chain study for common reed used in thatching
land_use_system: common reed; thatching; regional value chain
key_finding_short: Regional reed production could serve an existing import-dependent thatching value chain, but quality, harvest, processing and trade are decisive.
south_germany_relevance: Useful as value-chain example; direct market transfer to South Germany is uncertain.
caveat: Northern Germany and thatching-specific; not a general wet biomass pathway.

title: Transformation towards the sustainable management of peatlands: A characterisation of farmers in the Teufelsmoor, Germany
geometry_note: Approximate point for Teufelsmoor.
study_design: Qualitative interviews with peatland farmers; farmer typology
land_use_system: peatland farming; rewetting; farmer transition
key_finding_short: Farmers are not uniformly opposed to rewetting; support is conditional on planning security, income, ownership and trustful collaboration.
south_germany_relevance: Strong adoption source for explaining why compensation alone may be insufficient.
caveat: Teufelsmoor-specific; social and institutional context differs from Upper Swabia.

title: Readiness to transform: Pioneer farmers’ motivations and barriers for adopting paludiculture in Germany
region: Lower Saxony, Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania, Brandenburg, Bavaria, Schleswig-Holstein
geometry_note: Multi-region German study; approximate central display point. Includes Bavaria.
study_design: Qualitative interviews with pioneer farms; Health Belief Model
land_use_system: paludiculture; water buffalo; reed; peat moss; wet meadow hay; cattail
key_finding_short: Adoption is shaped by perceived threats from drainage, networks, research contacts, programmes and self-image; barriers include costs, regulation, markets and advice.
south_germany_relevance: Highly relevant because it includes German pioneer pathways and Bavarian cases.
caveat: Pioneer farms are not representative of mainstream farmers.

title: The integration of peatlands into the EU Common Agricultural Policy: Recent progress and remaining challenges
geometry_note: EU policy study; display at Brussels / EU node.
study_design: Comparative policy document analysis of EU Member States
land_use_system: peatland and wetland management; paludiculture eligibility; CAP
key_finding_short: CAP 2023–2027 creates a stronger framework for peat/wetland management, but implementation differs strongly between Member States.
south_germany_relevance: Important governance layer for understanding enabling and constraining policy conditions.
caveat: Policy framework source; does not quantify farm-level outcomes.

title: Ecological Basis of Ecosystem Services and Management of Wetlands Dominated by Common Reed (Phragmites australis): European Perspective
geometry_note: European review; use as Europe-level reed ecology node.
theme_block: Product pathways / reed ecology and management
study_design: Review of reed ecology, ecosystem services and management
land_use_system: common reed; wetlands; ecosystem services; management
key_finding_short: Reed can be habitat, raw material, water-treatment system or management problem depending on context and objectives.
south_germany_relevance: Useful for explaining that reed pathways require explicit management goals and context-specific design.
caveat: Ecological review; not a farm-economic or GHG mitigation study.

title: The Utilisation of Reed (Phragmites australis): A Review
geometry_note: Review; no single study site.
study_design: Descriptive review of reed use, harvest, yields and product pathways
land_use_system: reed; thatching; construction; insulation; paper; energy; fodder; compost; wastewater treatment
key_finding_short: Reed has multiple product pathways; harvest timing and quality requirements determine feasible uses.
south_germany_relevance: Useful background for product pathway exploration; not sufficient alone to justify implementation.
caveat: Older descriptive review; use as background rather than core evidence for economics or GHG.

title: The three-peat challenge: business as usual, responsible agriculture, and conservation and restoration as management trajectories in global peatlands
geometry_note: Conceptual/global synthesis; no single study region.
study_design: Conceptual synthesis
land_use_system: business as usual; responsible agriculture; conservation/restoration
key_finding_short: Frames peatland management as three trajectories: business as usual, responsible agriculture, and conservation/restoration.
south_germany_relevance: Very strong conceptual frame for the Atlas because it avoids a simplistic drainage-versus-restoration binary.
caveat: Not in the uploaded Top-20 file; added because it is central for the Transition Atlas framing.
